alt: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Altai.

fat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Fante Akan.
fault: noun. Culpability; the responsibility for a blameworthy event.
faun: noun. A woodland creature with pointed ears, legs, and short horns of a goat and a fondness for unrestrained revelry.
flak: noun. Ground - based anti-aircraft guns firing explosive shells.
flan: noun. A baked tart with a sweet or savoury filling in an open-topped pastry case.

hant: noun. Alternative form of haunt, haint (“ghost”)
hat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Haitian Creole.
hatful: noun. The amount that will fit into a hat.
haul: verb. To transport by drawing or pulling, as with horses or oxen, or a motor vehicle.
haunt: verb. To inhabit or to visit frequently (most often used in reference to ghosts).

khan: noun. A ruler over various Turkic and Mongol peoples in the Middle Ages.
khat: noun. A shrub, Catha edulis, whose leaves are used as a mild stimulant when chewed or brewed as tea; also a drug produced from this plant.
lank: adjective. Slender or thin; not well filled out; not plump; shrunken; lean.
lat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Latin.

nah: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Nahuan languages.
nth: adjective. Occurring at position n in a sequence.
nut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nùng.
talk: verb. To communicate, usually by means of speech.
taluk: noun. A hereditary estate in parts of India; subsequently, an administrative subdivision of a district.
tau: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Upper Tanana.
than: conjunction. Used in comparisons, to introduce the basis of comparison.
thank: noun. singular of thanks (“an expression of appreciation or gratitude; grateful feelings or thoughts; favour, goodwill, graciousness”)
uhlan: noun. A lancer, a soldier armed with a lance in a former light cavalry unit of the Polish, Prussian or German, Austrian, and Russian armies.

uta: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Otank.
